proposals.title: Hungarian checker: allow p-prefix on int params used as pointers
proposals.problem: analyze_function_completeness deducts points when parameters named with p-prefix (pUnit, pStatEntry) have storage type int instead of a pointer type. In D2Common.dll, ordinal exports receive all params as int but many are semantically pointers. This causes -3 per occurrence, affecting 3 of 7 documented functions (43%).
proposals.solution: In the Hungarian notation check within AnalysisService, add an exception: if prefix is 'p' and type is int/uint/undefined4, and the variable is a parameter (not local), treat as valid (pointer-passed-as-int pattern common in game DLLs).

evidence.score_impact: -3 per occurrence (up to -18 total across 5 functions)

implementation.description: Added isParameter boolean to validateHungarianNotation. When isParameter=true AND varName starts with 'p' followed by uppercase AND type is int/uint/undefined4/dword, skip violation. Parameters pass true, locals pass false.
implementation.changes_summary: validateHungarianNotation: +1 param (isParameter), +6 lines exception logic; 3 call sites updated

regression_result.note: Parameter-level p-prefix violations suppressed. Remaining violations are from decompiler-projected body symbols, correctly classified as unfixable.

proposals.title: Detect register-only SSA bodies early to skip variable rename step
proposals.problem: get_function_variables on worker bodies sometimes returns no locals because all variables are register-only SSA (bVar*, iVar*, piVar*, uVar*). The loop then attempts rename_variables which has no effect. This wastes 2 API calls per affected function.

implementation.description: Added 'has_renameable_variables' boolean field to analyze_function_completeness JSON response. Returns !localVarNames.isEmpty().

regression_result.note: Field correctly returns false for all 5 thunks tested (no renameable locals).
